title: ZPA Connector Health
spl:
index=network sourcetype="zscaler:connector"
| stats latest(status) as st by connector_name
| search st!="ONLINE"
description: Connector health is prerequisite for ZPA private application reachability.
value: Connector health is prerequisite for ZPA private application reachability.
implementation: Monitor private service edge and app connector heartbeats. Page on offline connectors affecting access.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Sessions.All_Sessions by All_Sessions.action, All_Sessions.src, All_Sessions.user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Zscaler blocks from category misclassifications, new SaaS rollouts, or short policy-tightening windows; triage with app owners and DLP before assuming compromise.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_zscaler`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=zscaler:connector`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Monitor private service edge and app connector heartbeats. Page on offline connectors affecting access.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="zscaler:connector"
| stats latest(status) as st by connector_name
| search st!="ONLINE"
```

Understanding this SPL

**ZPA Connector Health** ‚Äî Connector health is prerequisite for ZPA private application reachability.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=zscaler:connector`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_zscaler`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: zscaler:connector. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: Anomaly ‚Äî interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="zscaler:connector". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by connector_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Sessions.All_Sessions by All_Sessions.action, All_Sessions.src, All_Sessions.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**ZPA Connector Health** ‚Äî Connector health is prerequisite for ZPA private application reachability.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=zscaler:connector`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_zscaler`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: Anomaly ‚Äî interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Sessions.All_Sessions` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendor‚Äôs own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (connectors), Single value (offline count).
grandmaExplanation: We use Zscaler data and alerts to stay ahead of the risks described in "ZPA Connector Health". We help the team notice trouble early, while a situation is still small and containable.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Network_Sessions.All_Sessions` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
